overview: Dimir Control in Pauper focuses on surviving the early game with efficient removal and board wipes, then stabilizing with resilient creatures and card advantage. By leveraging signature cards like Snuff Out and Lorien Revealed, the deck clears opposing boards while maintaining its own resources. Tolarian Terror provides a durable, value-generating finisher that pressures opponents over time.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through card advantage and board control
Pressuring with resilient threats like Tolarian Terror
Winning via combat damage after clearing the board with Lorien Revealed

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction like Snuff Out
Mana bases that support two-color casting by turn three
Combinations of removal and a late-game threat
Keep any hand with 2-3 lands and at least one interaction spell (Snuff Out or Tolarian Terror).
Prioritize hands with 1-2 lands if they include Snuff Out, Tolarian Terror, and a cantrip or draw spell.
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws with three or more lands
Hands lacking any removal or board impact
Overloaded hands with only late-game cards
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands and no spells, or hands with 0-1 lands and no early interaction.

early_game: Play lands and hold up mana for Snuff Out
Trade early threats efficiently to preserve life total
Prioritize drawing into more removal and board wipes
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Snuff Out or Tolarian Terror if the opponent plays a 3+ mana creature or threat.
If no threat appears, play a draw spell or cheap cantrip to dig for answers.
if forced to mulligan to a 1-land, keep only if it includes Snuff Out and a cantrip.
mid_game: Cast Lorien Revealed to reset the board when ahead or at parity
Deploy Tolarian Terror to establish a persistent threat
Use card draw and filtering to find answers to opposing strategies
Preserve mana for interaction until turn 4-5.
Use cantrips to cycle through dead cards and find answers without losing tempo.
Don't overcommit to Lorien Revealed unless you have board control or protection.
Manage your graveyard carefully if facing graveyard hate
consider milling or sacrificing creatures to disrupt opponent plans.
Hold back on committing Lorien Revealed until you've answered the opponent's primary threat or have a way to protect it.
late_game: Maintain board presence with Tolarian Terror and other resilient creatures
Answer opposing finishers with remaining removal
Close out the game by trading up or attacking with a larger board
Lorien Revealed is the primary win condition, often paired with a resilient creature from hand or graveyard.
Snuff Out can flashback to clear a final blocker.
Tolarian Terror can counter a game-ending spell or remove a key threat.
Go all-in when you have answered the opponent's primary threat, have card advantage, and can protect Lorien Revealed or follow up with a creature.
Commit when you're ahead in resources and the opponent has no clear answer or protection.

key_cards.name: Tolarian Terror
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have card advantage or board control; its +1/+1 counter generation and flying make it difficult to remove without multiple answers.

key_cards.name: Snuff Out
key_cards.notes: Use to kill low-toughness threats or finish off damaged creatures; prioritize targets that would otherwise pressure your life total.

key_cards.name: Lorien Revealed
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have few or no permanents to clear the board; follow up with Tolarian Terror or other threats to regain initiative.

sequencing_tips: Hold up black mana for Snuff Out during opponent's turn to catch fast threats
Wait to cast Lorien Revealed until after opponents play their key threats or tap into combat
Cast Tolarian Terror on your turn when you can protect it or trade efficiently
Common mistake: Overcommitting to Lorien Revealed without board control.
Always have a plan B if your win condition is countered or removed.
Snuff Out's flashback is crucial for late-game removal, so don't waste it early.
Tolarian Terror's versatility makes it a key piece
use it wisely against combo and control.
Watch for format-specific interactions with graveyard strategies and adjust sequencing accordingly.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and life total preservation; use Lorien Revealed to reset aggressive boards.
Race to board wipes and value engines; avoid trading removal into unremovable threats.
Establish board presence early with Tolarian Terror; force opponents to use removal before casting board wipes.

Use flashback on Snuff Out to bypass counterspells. Chain cantrips to find alternative answers. Cast Lorien Revealed when you know they've used their counterspells. Consider casting threats in response to their spells to force unfavorable trades.

Prioritize early removal and counterspells to stop key ramp pieces. Use Tolarian Terror to counter mana accelerants. Don't let them get ahead in mana; apply pressure with cantrips and removal to disrupt their curve.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card draw and efficient removal. Use Tolarian Terror to counter their Lorien Revealed or key threats. Flashback Snuff Out to clear their board. Win by out-resourcing and better sequencing.

common_threats: Watch for mana bases that indicate ramp (Elves, Jund) or combo (Madness, Affinity).
Note when opponents hold up mana for protection spells or counterspells.
Track graveyard contents if they play graveyard synergies.
Pay attention to when they start casting their win conditions to time your interaction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Dimir Control archetype in Pauper format
